data.title: Bitcoin Miner MARA Reduces Workforce Amid Shift to AI and Digital Infrastructure
data.description: Bitcoin miner MARA has announced a reduction of approximately 15% of its workforce as part of a strategic shift towards artificial intelligence and digital infrastructure. According to NS3.AI, the affected employees will receive one month of paid leave along with 13 weeks of severance compensation. Additionally, MARA has entered into an agreement with Starwood to repurpose around 1 gigawatt of its Bitcoin mining infrastructure for AI workloads.

data.title: Asian Markets Open Higher as Nikkei and KOSPI Show Gains
data.description: Japan's Nikkei 225 index opened with a 1.17% increase on April 3, reflecting positive sentiment in the Asian markets. According to NS3.AI, South Korea's KOSPI also experienced a strong start, opening 2.77% higher. These gains indicate a favorable outlook for investors in the region, as both indices show upward momentum.

data.title: Polymarket: Over $5 million in LP grants to sports events in April
data.description: Polymarket official Mustafa (@mustafap0ly) posted on X that the platform will provide over $5 million in liquidity subsidies to the sports prediction market in April.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Wall Street's Gold Predictions: A Decade of Analysis Reveals Surprising Results
data.description: A comprehensive analysis of gold predictions over the past decade has yielded unexpected conclusions. According to PANews, the study involved comparing forecasts from historically accurate Wall Street institutions, prominent figures, and renowned analysts with actual outcomes. This meticulous examination aimed to identify the most precise predictors in the gold market. The findings challenge conventional wisdom and provide new insights into the accuracy of gold forecasting. The analysis highlights the complexities and uncertainties inherent in predicting precious metal trends, offering valuable lessons for investors and analysts alike.

data.title: Sources: FBI Director, Secretary of the Army, and Secretary of Labor may be leaving the government soon.
data.description: According to The Atlantic: Sources say that FBI Director Patel, Army Secretary Driscoll, and Labor Secretary Chavez-Dremer may soon leave President Trump's administration. (Jinshi)

data.title: Mining company MARA is laying off approximately 15% of its workforce and shifting its focus towards AI and digital infrastructure.
data.description: Bitcoin mining company MARA announced layoffs of approximately 15% of its workforce, affecting full-time employees across multiple departments and some contractors. CEO Fred Thiel stated in an internal memo that the layoffs were a strategic decision, as the company is focusing on new directions and the team structure needs to be adjusted accordingly. Affected employees will receive one month of paid leave and 13 weeks of severance pay. MARA had previously begun developing its AI and high-performance computing businesses. In February of this year, it completed a majority stake acquisition of Exaion, a data center subsidiary of French national energy company EDF, and reached an agreement with data center developer Starwood to convert approximately 1 gigawatt of Bitcoin mining infrastructure for AI workloads. MARA currently operates approximately 66.45 EH/s of hashrate, accounting for about 5% of the Bitcoin network's hashrate. The company recently sold over 15,000 BTC, worth approximately $1.1 billion, to repay $1 billion in convertible notes. MARA reports a net loss of $1.3 billion and adjusted EBITDA of -$330.8 million for 2025.

data.title: FBI Director and Other Officials May Depart Trump Administration
data.description: According to Odaily, sources indicate that U.S. President Donald Trump's administration may soon see the departure of several key officials. These include FBI Director Patel, Army Secretary Driscoll, and Labor Secretary Chavez-Dremer. The potential exits come amid ongoing changes within the administration.

data.title: Ethereum's Future: Exploring the 2026 Hard Fork
data.description: A recent report delves into the concept of 'Ethereum: One Chain to Rule Them All,' examining the upcoming hard fork scheduled for 2026. According to PANews, the report discusses the potential implications and changes that this significant update could bring to the Ethereum network. The hard fork is expected to address various technical and scalability issues, aiming to enhance the overall efficiency and functionality of the blockchain. As the Ethereum community anticipates these developments, the report highlights the importance of this transition in shaping the future of the network.

data.title: Japanese and South Korean stock markets opened higher.
data.description: According to Gate data, the Nikkei 225 index opened 615.75 points higher, or 1.17%, at 53,079.02 on Friday, April 3. The South Korean KOSPI index opened 145.0 points higher, or 2.77%, at 5,379.05 on Friday, April 3.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Acquires Coefficient Bio for $400 Million
data.description: Anthropic has completed the acquisition of startup Coefficient Bio for approximately $400 million. According to Odaily, this strategic move is expected to enhance Anthropic's capabilities in the AI sector. The acquisition reflects Anthropic's ongoing efforts to expand its technological footprint and strengthen its position in the competitive AI industry.

data.title: The president of the New York Federal Reserve warned that the oil price surge triggered by the war with Iran could have repercussions on the overall economy.
data.description: New York Federal Reserve President John Williams stated on FOX Business that the impact of the war with Iran on energy prices could spread to multiple sectors of the economy. Prices for various goods and services, including airfares, will rise due to increased fuel costs, a transmission process that typically takes months or even a year to fully materialize. Currently, the national average price of regular gasoline in the United States has exceeded $4 per gallon, an increase of over $1 since the outbreak of the war on February 28. Williams pointed out that rising energy prices both push up inflation and erode household disposable income, putting double pressure on economic demand. Williams stated that the Fed's current monetary policy is well-positioned to balance related risks, but the war with Iran is an unforeseen and sudden geopolitical shock, limiting the effectiveness of monetary policy in addressing oil price fluctuations. He emphasized the need for forward-looking decision-making, as the full impact of monetary policy on the economy takes at least a year to materialize.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Considers Cabinet Reshuffle Amid Discontent
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump is reportedly considering further changes to his cabinet due to dissatisfaction with Commerce Secretary Lutnick and Labor Secretary Dremer. According to BlockBeats, a government official stated that Trump is very angry and plans to make personnel adjustments.

Previously, U.S. Attorney General Bondi and Homeland Security Secretary Noem were dismissed. Discussions are ongoing regarding Dremer's position, while Lutnick's situation remains precarious. Although no final decisions have been made, insiders revealed that Trump had previously contemplated firing them but did not proceed. A larger-scale reshuffle would represent a significant restructuring of the federal government, targeting cabinet members who have underperformed or attracted excessive negative attention.

Removing Commerce Secretary Lutnick could signal Trump's intent to make changes on economic issues. However, a White House spokesperson affirmed that both officials are performing well and continue to receive the president's full support.

data.title: U.S. Adjusts Tariffs on Metal Imports to Simplify Processes
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump signed a statement on Thursday, local time, adjusting national security tariffs on steel, aluminum, and copper imports. According to BlockBeats, the changes aim to reduce tariff rates on derivative products, simplify declaration processes, and prevent misreporting of import values.

The statement outlines that the U.S. will maintain a 50% import tariff on commodities such as steel, aluminum, and copper, which will apply to the prices paid by American consumers. Additional changes include the removal of the 50% tariff on derivative products made from these metals, provided their metal content by weight is below 15%. This move will eliminate the Section 232 tariffs on products with minimal metal content.

U.S. officials noted that these adjustments are not expected to significantly impact the economy compared to the previous tariff system. However, imposing a 50% tariff on the total sales value of commodity metals may generate some additional tariff revenue.

data.title: Bitcoin Transfer from Cumberland DRW to Anonymous Address
data.description: A transfer of 44.61 BTC was recorded at 07:39. According to ChainCatcher, the Bitcoin, valued at approximately $2.2383 million, was moved from Cumberland DRW to an anonymous address starting with bc1qkgt0e.

data.title: Fed's Goolsbee: Rising Oil Prices Could Hamper Inflation Reduction Efforts
data.description: Federal Reserve official Austan Goolsbee expressed concerns over the timing of rising oil prices, stating that they could hinder efforts to reduce inflation. According to Jin10, Goolsbee noted that the increase in oil prices comes at a challenging time when there were hopes for inflation to ease. The Federal Reserve has been closely monitoring economic indicators to guide its monetary policy decisions, and the unexpected rise in oil prices adds complexity to the inflation outlook. Goolsbee's comments highlight the ongoing challenges faced by policymakers in balancing economic growth and inflation control.

data.title: Launch of Post-Quantum Blockchain Mainnet by Naoris Protocol
data.description: Naoris Protocol has introduced a post-quantum blockchain mainnet, utilizing cryptography sanctioned by the U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology. According to NS3.AI, this development occurs amidst concerns from developers that quantum computers might eventually compromise the signature systems employed by Bitcoin and Ethereum wallets. Transitioning to quantum-resistant cryptography could necessitate significant modifications across wallets, developer tools, and nodes.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 67,000 USDT with a Narrowed 1.61% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 02, 2026, 22:52 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 67,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 67,008 USDT, with a narrowed narrowed 1.61% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Tether Seeks Final Funding Push at $500 Billion Valuation
data.description: Tether is making a final push to secure funding at a valuation of $500 billion. According to ChainCatcher, the company is urging investors to commit to an agreement within the next two weeks.

data.title: Iran Conflict Costs Estimated at $30–45 Billion in Over a Month
data.description: The ongoing conflict in Iran is projected to cost between $30 billion and $45 billion in just over a month, translating to approximately $2.5 to $3.8 per person per day. According to NS3.AI, this financial burden is closely tied to increased U.S. military spending and a significant rise in oil prices, which have surged from around $79 to over $110 per barrel. The conflict has also contributed to higher fuel prices, inflation, and borrowing costs, impacting the global economy.
